mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales indicates conflicting demand signals; when housing starts accelerate while retail sales stagnate, it reflects a structural shift toward durable goods investment that boosts equity cash flows and drives positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of observations where the divergence condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the industrial production index relative to potential output signals capacity constraints that force firms to raise prices, expanding profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds 105.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO sustains levels above 105.0, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.04 despite the capacity constraint premise.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, which mechanically supports earnings per share growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis predicts positive returns but empirical forward returns are flat or negative in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, borrowing costs compress corporate leverage capacity and discount future cash flows aggressively, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or expand when the funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, or the forward return differential between high and low rate regimes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where both series confirm labor market strength.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the consumer price index signals disinflationary pressure that erodes nominal revenue growth expectations, prompting multiple compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change falls below 0.1 percent
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite confirmed disinflation, or the predictive power of CPI deceleration on forward returns vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 2-year rolling window.
